messages.content:
HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

Fix the hypothesis's targetNodes/perturbation so it passes validation WITHOUT changing the claim's meaning. If the claim cannot be expressed against funnel-active nodes, translate it into the funnel nodes it would actually move.

FUNNEL-ACTIVE NODES:
tamBase (EUR_M, value 300)
geo.DE (ratio, value 0.5)
geo.NL (ratio, value 0.28)
geo.PL (ratio, value 0.07)
geo.CH (ratio, value 0.08)
geo.CZ (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.AT (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.other (ratio, value 0.03)
seg.hyperscale (ratio, value 0.44)
seg.colocation (ratio, value 0.18)
seg.enterprise (ratio, value 0.29)
seg.edge (ratio, value 0.05)
seg.telecom (ratio, value 0.04)
cust.operator-large (ratio, value 0.4)
cust.operator-mid (ratio, value 0.25)
cust.oem (ratio, value 0.22)
cust.distributor (ratio, value 0.13)
serviceableFactor (ratio, value 0.55)
obtainableFactor (ratio, value 0.01)

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "id": "intelligent-pdu-concentration-shrinks-serviceable",
 "title": "In the intelligent-PDU sub-market the venture actually plays in, Legrand's dual acquisition plus DCIM spec-lock makes concentration — and the serviceable haircut — worse than the headline",
 "narrative": "shape.cr3 = 0.55 is computed for the whole rack-PDU category, and serviceableFactor's 0.55 haircut is justified purely on regulatory reach (EN 50600, ecodesign, CE/TÜV). Neither number describes the venture's actual product market: INTELLIGENT PDUs, where Legrand owns both specialist brands (Raritan and Server Technology), Vertiv holds Geist, and Schneider leads the category — so the intelligent sub-segment is materially more concentrated than the blended 0.55 suggests. Concentration in intelligent PDUs is not just competitive pressure; it is a serviceability constraint, because intelligent-PDU purchases are spec-locked to DCIM/monitoring ecosystems and vendor-certified compatibility lists. Demand written into specs as 'Raritan-compatible' or 'certified for our DCIM' is not reachable at any price. The 0.55 serviceableFactor, built only on regulatory logic, silently assumes all regulatory-clear demand is spec-open — the one assumption the sub-market's ownership structure contradicts.",
 "category": "model-structure",
 "targetNodes": [
  "serviceableFactor"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "Legrand consolidates the intelligent-PDU specialist brands (Raritan AND Server Technology) while Vertiv holds Geist — the intelligent sub-segment's effective concentration exceeds the blended rack-PDU CR3 of 0.55, which is itself extrapolated from a single 15.8% datapoint",
   "refs": [
    "digest:cr3-vendors#c4",
    "digest:cr3-vendors#c0",
    "ledger:shape.cr3"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "Intelligent-PDU procurement is written against DCIM/monitoring ecosystems: buyer's guides treat DCIM compatibility as a primary selection criterion and the intelligent-PDU sub-market carries only qualitative 'Medium' concentration labels with no share table (Omdia's vendor-share data is paywalled) — so the spec-locked fraction of demand is real but unmeasured",
   "refs": [
    "digest:cust-mix#c13",
    "digest:cr3-vendors#c12",
    "digest:cr3-vendors#c10"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "serviceableFactor's 0.55, derived solely from the regulatory stack, omits an ecosystem-compatibility haircut; applying it drops effective serviceable share toward 0.40 — the venture's SAM overstates reachable demand even where every certificate is in hand",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:serviceableFactor",
    "profile:Product"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "The team has a serviceable haircut AND a concentration figure, so both boxes look ticked. What hides the risk is scope mismatch: the CR3 describes a broader category than the product, the serviceableFactor describes a narrower constraint (regulation) than reality, and no ledger node connects them — the DCIM spec-lock falls exactly into the seam between two nodes that each look individually handled.",
 "expectedObviousness": "medium",
 "falsifier": "The Omdia rack-PDU vendor-share table showing intelligent-PDU top-3 combined share below ~50%, or ≥3 CE operator/colo PDU specifications requiring only open protocols (SNMP/Redfish/Modbus) with no vendor-certified DCIM compatibility list.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.35,
  "rationale": "The ownership consolidation is documented fact and DCIM compatibility is a documented selection criterion, but the actual spec-locked fraction of CE demand is unmeasured — the perturbation size rests on judgment over hard shares, which the paywalled Omdia table could resolve either way.",
  "basis": "evidence"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "serviceableFactor",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 0.4,
   "note": "Regulatory-clear but DCIM/spec-locked demand removed from the serviceable share"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "DCIM/compatibility language in CE colo and enterprise PDU specifications",
   "where": "TED tender technical annexes; colo operator technical-standards documents (Equinix/NTT design guides); Data Centre World CE conference specs sessions",
   "threshold": "Majority of sampled specs naming vendor-certified DCIM compatibility rather than open protocols",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "Legrand intelligent-PDU portfolio integration moves (Raritan/Server Technology product-line merges, unified DCIM certification programs)",
   "where": "Legrand investor day materials; Raritan and Server Technology product announcements",
   "threshold": "Announcement of a unified compatibility/certification program spanning both brands",
   "updates": "increases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Purchase the Omdia Rack PDU Annual Market Analysis (2024 base, vendor shares) to measure intelligent-PDU concentration directly",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "serviceableFactor",
   "note": "The one named source that converts the qualitative 'Medium' label into shares, sizing the spec-locked haircut"
  },
  {
   "action": "Engineer and certify against open standards (Redfish/SNMP) and the top two third-party DCIM platforms before launch, converting spec-locked demand into reachable demand",
   "type": "strategic",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": "Compatibility certification is a buyable asset, unlike incumbent install base"
  },
  {
   "action": "Sample 20 recent CE PDU specifications and count spec-open vs vendor-locked language to bound the ecosystem haircut empirically",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "serviceableFactor",
   "note": "Cheap bottom-up cross-check the ledger itself flags as missing for this node"
  }
 ],
 "lens": "competitive-foreclosure",
 "riskId": "risk.competitive-foreclosure.intelligent-pdu-concentration-shrinks-serviceable"
}

VALIDATION ERROR:
[
  {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "values": [
      "error",
      "risk"
    ],
    "path": [
      0,
      "resolution"
    ],
    "message": "Invalid option: expected one of \"error\"|\"risk\""
  }
]

Emit the corrected hypothesis JSON (same shape).
